title: LOOM: The Bells of Basin City & Lore (solo RPG)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=m1gcxwLGPos

A Boxing Day return to one of the most distinctive worlds in solo gaming: Daniel (@dungeondive) reviews LOOM: The Bells of Basin City by J. Merritt of Shield Dice Studio, alongside the LOOM lore book — a setting tome he argues deserves the same boutique reverence as Vermis, right down to the cipher keyboard for decoding hidden sigils across the series. LOOM emulates an 80s text-adventure on your tabletop: a command list of verbs, encounters built from card-drawn mad-lib paragraphs, and prose Daniel compares to Clark Ashton Smith. This second volume casts you as a Defacer — a rogue witch-assassin crossing the rooftops of Basin City to kill the Empress, tagging buildings with magical graffiti to stoke rebellion while four bells track how close the city is to catching you. There are no hit points this time; it's resource management — warmth, city influence, a shape-shifting Pagan Key — and a Rogue Legacy-style system passes items from dead characters to their successors. The play session is a delight of atmospheric failure: hunted by the lumbering Hammer of the Lutheran, crashing through a rooftop, freezing across flesh-canvas awnings with hollow eyes watching from the folds, then scrawling an Ember Light sigil that glows like the blood of a cosmic beast. "Death to the Empress. That's so metal."

----

LOOM's world spans four regions and only two have gamebooks so far. What half-revealed game world do you most want its creator to finish mapping?
